chain_edges.notes: 5-pole exclusive lab-cloud alliance regime directly feeds the three-cloud training oligopoly thesis.
Training-vs-inference SKU split underwrites the layered training/inference market shape.
Three-cloud frontier-training capture reinforces closed-weight frontier vs open-weight production split.
Inference-tier specialization by Oracle/IBM/Coreweave dovetails with cloud-hosted Robot-as-a-Service inference.

relations.notes: Both describe lab-cloud structural lock-in; this prediction emphasizes capacity oligopoly, the other emphasizes 5-pole exclusivity.
Three-cloud training oligopoly and training-vs-inference SKU split are independent facets of the same hyperscaler silicon stack.
Capacity-driven cloud oligopoly and proprietary closed-weights split share Q3 2026 horizon but cover different layers.
Hyperscaler control-plane race and frontier training-capacity capture are independent but mutually reinforcing oligopoly axes.
Cloud-hosted robot training pipeline and three-cloud frontier-training capture both reflect compute centralization in the same Q3 2026 window.
